docs.title: Petstore gRPC
docs.text: A Python gRPC service implementing a pet store API with health check endpoint.

docs.title: Features
docs.text:
• gRPC API — High-performance RPC framework with Protocol Buffers
  • Health Check — Service status and build metadata endpoint
  • Modern Python — Built with Python 3.14, async/await, and type hints
  • Developer Experience — Fast iteration with uv, ruff linting, and comprehensive tests
  • Production Ready — Multi-stage Docker build, CI/CD, and monitoring hooks

docs.title: Prerequisites
docs.text:
• Python 3.14+
  • uv package manager

docs.title: Installation
docs.text:
# Clone the repository
git clone https://github.com/RamonAlcantaraArceo/petstore-grpc.git
cd petstore-grpc

# Install dependencies
uv sync

docs.title: Running Locally
docs.text:
# Start the server
uv run python -m petstore_grpc

# In another terminal, test the health endpoint
grpcurl -plaintext -d '{}' localhost:50051 petstore.v1.Health/Check

docs.title: Running with Docker
docs.text:
# Build and run with docker compose
docker compose up --build

# Test the endpoint
grpcurl -plaintext -d '{}' localhost:50051 petstore.v1.Health/Check

docs.title: Running Tests
docs.text: uv run pytest

docs.title: Linting and Formatting
docs.text:
# Check formatting
uv run ruff check .
uv run ruff format --check .

# Auto-fix issues
uv run ruff check --fix .
uv run ruff format .

docs.title: Regenerating Proto Stubs
docs.text:
After modifying .proto files:

bash scripts/gen_protos.sh

docs.title: Documentation
docs.text: Full documentation is available at the project docs site.

docs.title: Contributing with Claude Code
docs.text: This repository includes Claude Code agent configurations for enhanced development workflow. See CLAUDE.md in the repository for details on using the agent effectively.

docs.title: License
docs.text: MIT

docs.title: Architecture
docs.text: This document describes the high-level architecture and design decisions for petstore-grpc.

docs.title: 1. Proto Definitions (proto/petstore/v1/)
docs.text:
Protocol Buffer schemas define the API contract:

  • health.proto — Health check service and messages

Generated Python stubs are committed to the repository in src/petstore_grpc/generated/. This simplifies CI and eliminates build-time codegen.

docs.title: 2. Server Bootstrap (server.py)
docs.text:
The main server lifecycle manager:

  • Creates grpc.aio.server() for async-first design
  • Registers all servicers (currently HealthServicer)
  • Binds to 0.0.0.0:${PORT} (default 50051)
  • Handles SIGTERM/SIGINT for graceful shutdown with 5s grace period

docs.title: 3. Health Service (services/health.py)
docs.text:
Implements the Health/Check RPC:

Response fields:

  • status — Always "SERVING" (future: readiness checks)
  • mode — Runtime mode from MODE env var (dev, prod, staging)
  • details.version — Package version via importlib.metadata
  • details.build_date — Build timestamp (injected by Docker, defaults to "unknown")
  • details.git_commit_sha — Git commit SHA (injected by Docker, defaults to "unknown")

docs.title: 4. Configuration (config.py)
docs.text:
Environment variable loader with caching:

  • Settings dataclass (frozen) holds all config
  • get_settings() uses @lru_cache for singleton pattern
  • Tests can clear cache to inject custom env vars

docs.title: 5. Entry Point (__main__.py)
docs.text:
Simple entry point for python -m petstore_grpc:

  • Configures logging
  • Calls asyncio.run(serve())

docs.title: Async-First with grpc.aio
docs.text:
All server and servicer code uses async/await:

  • Benefits: Non-blocking I/O, efficient concurrency, native asyncio integration
  • Trade-offs: Slightly more complex than sync code, but better scalability

docs.title: Generated Stubs Committed
docs.text:
Proto-generated Python files are checked into version control:

  • Pros: No codegen in CI/Docker, faster builds, explicit diffs
  • Cons: Manual regeneration required after .proto changes
  • Mitigation: CI job checks for drift (git diff --exit-code)

docs.title: Version from __init__.py
docs.text:
Hatchling reads __version__ from src/petstore_grpc/__init__.py:

  • Single source of truth
  • Simple to update (just edit the string)
  • Runtime lookup via importlib.metadata.version("petstore-grpc")

docs.title: Build Metadata via Docker ARG/ENV
docs.text:
Build date and git commit are injected at Docker build time:

  • ARG BUILD_DATE / ENV BUILD_DATE
  • ARG GIT_COMMIT_SHA / ENV GIT_COMMIT_SHA
  • Defaults to "unknown" for local dev (no Docker)

docs.title: uv for Dependency Management
docs.text:
Uses the fast uv package manager:

  • pyproject.toml + uv.lock for reproducible installs
  • uv sync instead of pip install
  • Faster than pip, especially in Docker

docs.title: Ruff-Only Linting
docs.text:
Consolidates formatting and linting into a single tool:

  • Replaces black, isort, flake8, and more
  • Google-style docstrings enforced via pydocstyle rules
  • Generated code excluded via per-file-ignores

docs.title: Directory Structure
docs.text:
petstore-grpc/
├── src/petstore_grpc/         # Source code
│   ├── __init__.py            # Package + version
│   ├── __main__.py            # Entry point
│   ├── server.py              # gRPC server
│   ├── config.py              # Environment config
│   ├── services/              # RPC implementations
│   │   └── health.py
│   └── generated/             # Proto stubs (committed)
│       └── petstore/v1/
├── tests/                     # Test suite
│   ├── conftest.py            # Shared fixtures
│   ├── unit/                  # Fast, isolated tests
│   └── integration/           # Full gRPC tests
├── proto/                     # Proto definitions
│   └── petstore/v1/
├── scripts/                   # Dev tooling
│   └── gen_protos.sh
├── docs/                      # Documentation (mkdocs)
├── .github/workflows/         # CI/CD
├── .claude/                   # Agent configurations
├── Dockerfile                 # Multi-stage build
├── docker-compose.yml         # Local orchestration
└── pyproject.toml             # Python project metadata

docs.title: Readiness Checks
docs.text:
Currently status is hard-coded to "SERVING". Future work:

  • Add dependency health checks (database, external APIs)
  • Return "NOT_SERVING" if dependencies are unavailable

docs.title: Observability
docs.text:
Potential integrations:

  • OpenTelemetry for distributed tracing
  • Prometheus metrics export
  • Structured logging (JSON) for production

docs.title: Additional Services
docs.text:
The architecture supports adding more gRPC services:

  1. Define .proto in proto/petstore/v1/
  2. Regenerate stubs via scripts/gen_protos.sh
  3. Implement servicer in src/petstore_grpc/services/
  4. Register in server.py
  5. Add tests in tests/

docs.title: Non-Root Container
docs.text:
Dockerfile creates and runs as user appuser (UID 1000):

RUN useradd -m -u 1000 appuser
USER appuser

docs.title: No Secrets in Env Vars
docs.text:
Build metadata (BUILD_DATE, GIT_COMMIT_SHA) is non-sensitive. For secrets:

  • Use Docker secrets or Kubernetes secrets
  • Never log or expose in health responses

docs.title: Input Validation
docs.text:
gRPC automatically validates message types via protobuf. For custom validation:

  • Add checks in servicer methods
  • Return grpc.StatusCode.INVALID_ARGUMENT for bad input

docs.title: Running the Server
docs.text: This guide covers different ways to run the petstore-grpc server.

docs.title: Using uv (Recommended)
docs.text:
The fastest way to run the server locally:

uv run python -m petstore_grpc

This starts the server on localhost:50051 in development mode.

docs.title: Configuration
docs.text:
The server reads configuration from environment variables:

Variable Default Description PORT 50051 gRPC server port MODE dev Runtime mode (dev, prod, staging) BUILD_DATE unknown Build timestamp (set by Docker) GIT_COMMIT_SHA unknown Git commit SHA (set by Docker)

Example with custom configuration:

MODE=staging PORT=9090 uv run python -m petstore_grpc

docs.title: Using Docker Compose
docs.text:
The recommended way to run in a containerized environment:

# Build and start
docker compose up --build

# Run in detached mode
docker compose up -d

# View logs
docker compose logs -f

# Stop
docker compose down

docs.title: Building with Build Metadata
docs.text:
To inject build metadata (timestamp and git commit):

docker compose build \
  --build-arg BUILD_DATE=$(date -u +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ) \
  --build-arg GIT_COMMIT_SHA=$(git rev-parse HEAD)

docker compose up

docs.title: Using Docker Directly
docs.text:
# Build
docker build -t petstore-grpc:latest .

# Run
docker run -p 50051:50051 petstore-grpc:latest

docs.title: Testing the Server
docs.text:
Once the server is running, test the Health endpoint using grpcurl:

# Install grpcurl if needed
# macOS: brew install grpcurl
# Linux: https://github.com/fullstorydev/grpcurl/releases

# Call the health check
grpcurl -plaintext -d '{}' localhost:50051 petstore.v1.Health/Check

Expected response:

{
  "status": "SERVING",
  "mode": "dev",
  "details": {
    "version": "0.1.0",
    "buildDate": "unknown",
    "gitCommitSha": "unknown"
  }
}

docs.title: Port Already in Use
docs.text:
If port 50051 is already in use, change it via the PORT environment variable:

PORT=50052 uv run python -m petstore_grpc

docs.title: Import Errors
docs.text:
If you see import errors, ensure dependencies are installed:

uv sync

docs.title: Proto Stubs Missing
docs.text:
If you see errors about missing health_pb2 modules, regenerate the stubs:

bash scripts/gen_protos.sh

docs.title: Running the Tests
docs.text: This guide explains how to run the test suite and interpret results.

docs.title: Running All Tests
docs.text:
uv run pytest

This runs all unit and integration tests with coverage reporting.

docs.title: By Directory
docs.text:
# Unit tests only
uv run pytest tests/unit/

# Integration tests only
uv run pytest tests/integration/

docs.title: By File
docs.text: uv run pytest tests/unit/test_health.py

docs.title: By Test Name
docs.text: uv run pytest tests/unit/test_health.py::test_health_check_returns_serving_status

docs.title: Terminal Output
docs.text:
Coverage is displayed in the terminal after tests complete:

uv run pytest

docs.title: HTML Report
docs.text:
Generate a detailed HTML coverage report:

uv run pytest --cov-report=html
open htmlcov/index.html  # macOS
xdg-open htmlcov/index.html  # Linux

docs.title: Coverage Configuration
docs.text:
Coverage reporting is configured in pyproject.toml:

[tool.pytest.ini_options]
addopts = "-ra --strict-markers --cov=petstore_grpc"

If you want to enforce a minimum threshold, add --cov-fail-under=<percent> to addopts.

docs.title: Unit Tests (tests/unit/)
docs.text:
Fast, isolated tests that mock external dependencies:

  • test_health.py — Health servicer logic with mocked gRPC context

docs.title: Integration Tests (tests/integration/)
docs.text:
End-to-end tests with real gRPC channels:

  • test_server_startup.py — Full server lifecycle and RPC invocation

docs.title: Fixtures (tests/conftest.py)
docs.text:
Shared test infrastructure:

  • grpc_server — In-process server on ephemeral port
  • grpc_channel — Client channel connected to test server

docs.title: Unit Test Example
docs.text:
import pytest
from unittest.mock import MagicMock
from petstore_grpc.services.health import HealthServicer
from petstore_grpc.generated.petstore.v1 import health_pb2

@pytest.mark.asyncio
async def test_my_feature():
    servicer = HealthServicer()
    request = health_pb2.HealthRequest()
    context = MagicMock()

    response = await servicer.Check(request, context)

    assert response.status == "SERVING"

docs.title: Integration Test Example
docs.text:
import pytest
from petstore_grpc.generated.petstore.v1 import health_pb2, health_pb2_grpc

@pytest.mark.asyncio
async def test_end_to_end(grpc_channel):
    stub = health_pb2_grpc.HealthStub(grpc_channel)
    request = health_pb2.HealthRequest()

    response = await stub.Check(request)

    assert response.status == "SERVING"

docs.title: Continuous Integration
docs.text:
Tests run automatically on every push and pull request via GitHub Actions:

  • Lint — Ruff check and format validation
  • Markdown — Prettier formatting check
  • Test — Full pytest suite with coverage
  • Proto Check — Verify generated stubs are up to date

See .github/workflows/ci.yml in the repository for the full CI pipeline.

docs.title: Async Tests Failing
docs.text:
Ensure pytest-asyncio is installed and configured:

[tool.pytest.ini_options]
asyncio_mode = "auto"

docs.title: Import Errors
docs.text:
Clear Python cache and reinstall:

find . -type d -name __pycache__ -exec rm -r {} +
uv sync

docs.title: Port Conflicts
docs.text: Integration tests use ephemeral ports, so conflicts are rare. If you see port errors, ensure no other tests are running concurrently.

docs.title: Health Check
docs.text:
Use this command to confirm the server is reachable over gRPC and reflection is enabled.

grpcurl -plaintext -d '{}' localhost:50051 petstore.v1.Health/Check

Expected response:

{
  "status": "SERVING",
  "mode": "dev",
  "details": {
    "version": "0.1.0",
    "build_date": "unknown",
    "git_commit_sha": "unknown"
  }
}

If you pass any fields, grpcurl will return a validation error because HealthRequest is empty.

docs.title: FindPetsByStatus
docs.text: Use the memory debug launch profile with SEED_DATASET=basic or mixed_v1 for these examples.

docs.title: Find pets by status
docs.text:
grpcurl -plaintext -d '{}' localhost:50051 petstore.v1.PetService/FindPetsByStatus

To filter explicitly:

grpcurl -plaintext -d '{"status":"PET_STATUS_AVAILABLE"}' localhost:50051 petstore.v1.PetService/FindPetsByStatus

docs.title: Find pets by tags
docs.text: grpcurl -plaintext -d '{"tags":["friendly","trained"]}' localhost:50051 petstore.v1.PetService/FindPetsByTags

docs.title: Get a pet by ID
docs.text: grpcurl -plaintext -d '{"petId":"1"}' localhost:50051 petstore.v1.PetService/GetPetById

docs.title: Add a pet
docs.text: grpcurl -plaintext -d '{"pet":{"name":"Ollie","photoUrls":["https://example.com/ollie.png"],"status":"PET_STATUS_AVAILABLE"}}' localhost:50051 petstore.v1.PetService/AddPet

docs.title: Update a pet
docs.text: grpcurl -plaintext -d '{"pet":{"id":"1","name":"Buddy Prime","photoUrls":["https://example.com/buddy.png"],"status":"PET_STATUS_AVAILABLE"}}' localhost:50051 petstore.v1.PetService/UpdatePet

docs.title: Update a pet with form fields
docs.text: grpcurl -plaintext -d '{"petId":"1","name":"Buddy Updated","status":"available"}' localhost:50051 petstore.v1.PetService/UpdatePetWithForm

docs.title: Delete a pet
docs.text: grpcurl -plaintext -d '{"petId":"1"}' localhost:50051 petstore.v1.PetService/DeletePet

docs.title: Upload file
docs.text: UploadFile is defined as a client-streaming RPC, but it is currently not implemented by the server.

docs.title: Notes
docs.text:
• petId, photoUrls, and other field names use grpcurl's JSON form of the proto field names.
  • For FindPetsByTags, use a seeded dataset that includes tags, such as mixed_v1.

docs.title: Store Service grpcurl Samples
docs.text: The store service uses the DB-backed launch profile. Start the server with the VS Code configuration named petstore-grpc: debug database, or set STORAGE_MODE=local and a valid DATABASE_URL before running uv run python -m petstore_grpc.

docs.title: Get inventory
docs.text: grpcurl -plaintext -d '{}' localhost:50051 petstore.v1.StoreService/GetInventory

docs.title: Place an order
docs.text: grpcurl -plaintext -d '{"order":{"petId":"1","quantity":1,"status":"ORDER_STATUS_PLACED","complete":false}}' localhost:50051 petstore.v1.StoreService/PlaceOrder

docs.title: Get an order by ID
docs.text: grpcurl -plaintext -d '{"orderId":"1"}' localhost:50051 petstore.v1.StoreService/GetOrderById

docs.title: Delete an order
docs.text: grpcurl -plaintext -d '{"orderId":"1"}' localhost:50051 petstore.v1.StoreService/DeleteOrder

docs.title: User Service grpcurl Samples
docs.text: The user service also uses the DB-backed launch profile. Start the server with petstore-grpc: debug database, or run with STORAGE_MODE=local and a valid DATABASE_URL.

docs.title: Create a user
docs.text: grpcurl -plaintext -d '{"user":{"username":"jane.doe","email":"jane@example.com","firstName":"Jane","lastName":"Doe"},"password":"secret"}' localhost:50051 petstore.v1.UserService/CreateUser

docs.title: Create multiple users
docs.text: grpcurl -plaintext -d '{"users":[{"user":{"username":"alice","email":"alice@example.com"},"password":"secret"},{"user":{"username":"bob","email":"bob@example.com"},"password":"secret"}]}' localhost:50051 petstore.v1.UserService/CreateUsersWithList

docs.title: Get a user by name
docs.text: grpcurl -plaintext -d '{"username":"jane.doe"}' localhost:50051 petstore.v1.UserService/GetUserByName

docs.title: Update a user
docs.text: grpcurl -plaintext -d '{"username":"jane.doe","user":{"username":"jane.doe","email":"jane+new@example.com"},"password":"new-secret"}' localhost:50051 petstore.v1.UserService/UpdateUser

docs.title: Delete a user
docs.text: grpcurl -plaintext -d '{"username":"jane.doe"}' localhost:50051 petstore.v1.UserService/DeleteUser

docs.title: Login and logout
docs.text:
grpcurl -plaintext -d '{"username":"jane.doe","password":"secret"}' localhost:50051 petstore.v1.UserService/LoginUser
grpcurl -plaintext -d '{}' localhost:50051 petstore.v1.UserService/LogoutUser

These RPCs are currently stubbed out by the server and return UNIMPLEMENTED.

docs.text: Field Type Label Description status string "SERVING" mode string e.g. "memory", "local", "cloud" details HealthResponse.Details

docs.text: Field Type Label Description version string build_date string git_commit_sha string

docs.title: Health
docs.text: Method Name Request Type Response Type Description Check HealthRequest HealthResponse

docs.title: Scalar Value Types
docs.text: .proto Type Notes C++ Java Python Go C# PHP Ruby  double double double float float64 double float Float  float float float float float32 float float Float  int32 Uses variable-length encoding. Inefficient for encoding negative numbers – if your field is likely to have negative values, use sint32 instead. int32 int int int32 int integer Bignum or Fixnum (as required)  int64 Uses variable-length encoding. Inefficient for encoding negative numbers – if your field is likely to have negative values, use sint64 instead. int64 long int/long int64 long integer/string Bignum  uint32 Uses variable-length encoding. uint32 int int/long uint32 uint integer Bignum or Fixnum (as required)  uint64 Uses variable-length encoding. uint64 long int/long uint64 ulong integer/string Bignum or Fixnum (as required)  sint32 Uses variable-length encoding. Signed int value. These more efficiently encode negative numbers than regular int32s. int32 int int int32 int integer Bignum or Fixnum (as required)  sint64 Uses variable-length encoding. Signed int value. These more efficiently encode negative numbers than regular int64s. int64 long int/long int64 long integer/string Bignum  fixed32 Always four bytes. More efficient than uint32 if values are often greater than 2^28. uint32 int int uint32 uint integer Bignum or Fixnum (as required)  fixed64 Always eight bytes. More efficient than uint64 if values are often greater than 2^56. uint64 long int/long uint64 ulong integer/string Bignum  sfixed32 Always four bytes. int32 int int int32 int integer Bignum or Fixnum (as required)  sfixed64 Always eight bytes. int64 long int/long int64 long integer/string Bignum  bool bool boolean boolean bool bool boolean TrueClass/FalseClass  string A string must always contain UTF-8 encoded or 7-bit ASCII text. string String str/unicode string string string String (UTF-8)  bytes May contain any arbitrary sequence of bytes. string ByteString str []byte ByteString string String (ASCII-8BIT)
